Project.NAME: Modular-Local-TTS-Runtime
Project.keywords: TTS, text-to-speech, Qwen3, OmniVoice, Piper, Apple Silicon, Windows, Linux, MLX, PyTorch, ONNX, FastAPI, Telegram bot, CLI, remote client, voice synthesis, voice cloning, voice design, audio generation, model inference
Project.annotation: Phase 1 central-server text-to-speech runtime with shared core services, the HTTP server as the sole runtime and model host, Telegram as a remote HTTP client, and CLI kept outside Phase 1 unless a blocker appears.

M-CONFIG.NAME: CoreSettings
M-CONFIG.purpose: Parse and validate environment-based runtime configuration for all components.
M-CONFIG.implementation-note: Runtime configuration uses the project-wide `TTS_*` namespace as the only supported environment contract; env parsing is owned by the pydantic-settings model `CoreEnvSettings`, while `parse_core_settings_from_env` projects the validated model into the legacy `CoreSettingsEnv` TypedDict for downstream consumers.

class-CoreSettings.PURPOSE: Frozen dataclass holding all shared runtime settings

class-CoreEnvSettings.PURPOSE: Pydantic-settings model that owns the canonical TTS_* environment contract

fn-parse_core_settings_from_env.PURPOSE: Parse environment variables into typed settings dict

fn-runtime_capability_map.PURPOSE: Expose the active runtime capability bindings independent of on-disk artifact presence

fn-resolve_runtime_model_binding.PURPOSE: Resolve the runtime-bound model identifier for a requested execution mode

type-CoreSettingsEnv.PURPOSE: TypedDict describing the parsed settings shape

M-ERRORS.NAME: DomainErrors
M-ERRORS.purpose: Define typed domain error hierarchy for all core operations.

class-CoreError.PURPOSE: Base exception for all domain errors

class-RuntimeCapabilityNotConfiguredErro.PURPOSE: Requested runtime capability has no active model binding

M-OBSERVABILITY.NAME: Observability
M-OBSERVABILITY.purpose: Provide request context propagation, structured logging, and timing utilities.

fn-log_event.PURPOSE: Emit structured JSON log with request context

fn-bind_request_context.PURPOSE: Set request ID in context var

fn-get_request_id.PURPOSE: Read current request ID from context

class-OperationScope.PURPOSE: Context manager for operation name propagation

M-METRICS.NAME: OperationalMetrics
M-METRICS.purpose: Collect and expose counters, gauges, and timing metrics for runtime operations.

M-CONTRACTS.NAME: DTOs
M-CONTRACTS.purpose: Define command DTOs, job contracts, and result types for the TTS domain.

M-MODELS.NAME: ModelMetadata
M-MODELS.purpose: Define current manifest-backed model metadata, rollout state, backend artifact validation rules, and the composite manifest loader that merges per-family fragments and .models/ auto-discovery into a single ModelManifest.
M-MODELS.implementation-note: Phase 2.7 introduces `core/models/composite.py` with `load_composite_manifest(...)` which loads the base `manifest.v1.json`, walks an optional fragments directory (`core/models/manifests/*.json` by default) and an optional `.models/`-style directory for per-folder `model_manifest.json` files, then merges every fragment's `models[]` entries into the base before re-running `ModelManifest.from_mapping(...)` validation. Fragment files accept three shapes: `{"models": [...]}`, `{"model": {...}}`, or a single model object with `key` + `folder`. Existing call sites continue to use `load_model_manifest(...)`; Phase 2.8 will switch bootstrap to call the composite loader.

class-ModelSpec.PURPOSE: Typed model specification for current catalog entries

class-CompositeManifestError.PURPOSE: Raised on composite manifest merge failures

fn-load_model_manifest.PURPOSE: Parse and validate the single base manifest file from disk

fn-get_model_manifest.PURPOSE: Expose the active model manifest to registry consumers

fn-load_composite_manifest.PURPOSE: Build a single manifest by merging the base manifest with per-family fragments and .models/ auto-discovery

fn-discover_model_manifests.PURPOSE: Yield (label, payload) pairs for every JSON fragment found in fragments_dir + models_dir

M-EXECUTION-PLAN.NAME: ExecutionPlanContracts
M-EXECUTION-PLAN.purpose: Define normalized capability, request, and execution-plan types for planned synthesis orchestration; capabilities are now an open string alias backed by the dynamic CapabilityRegistry instead of a closed Literal.
M-EXECUTION-PLAN.implementation-note: Phase 3.10 broadens `SynthesisCapability` from a closed `Literal[...]` to an open `str` alias. The forward (`capability_to_execution_mode`) and reverse (`execution_mode_to_capability`) helpers now defer to `core.contracts.capabilities.DEFAULT_CAPABILITY_REGISTRY`, so introducing a new capability (e.g. `emotion_transfer`) only requires registering a new `CapabilitySpec` with the registry — no edits to `core/contracts/synthesis.py`. The three built-in capabilities (`preset_speaker_tts`/`custom`, `voice_description_tts`/`design`, `reference_voice_clone`/`clone`) remain pre-registered at module load.

type-SynthesisCapability.PURPOSE: Open string alias for synthesis capability identifiers; concrete validation lives in DEFAULT_CAPABILITY_REGISTRY

class-SynthesisRequest.PURPOSE: Normalized request shape used by planner and coordinator

class-ExecutionPlan.PURPOSE: Resolved family, model, backend, and execution parameters

M-CAPABILITIES.NAME: CapabilityRegistry
M-CAPABILITIES.purpose: Provide a process-local registry that owns the capability <-> execution_mode mapping so new model families can introduce new capabilities at runtime without editing the closed Literal that previously lived in core/contracts/synthesis.py.
M-CAPABILITIES.implementation-note: Phase 3.10 introduces `core/contracts/capabilities.py` with a frozen `CapabilitySpec(name, execution_mode, description, aliases)` dataclass and a `CapabilityRegistry` class that exposes `register(spec)`, `get(name)`, `is_supported(name)`, `execution_mode_for(name)`, `capability_for_mode(mode)`, `names()`, `execution_modes()`, and `specs()`. The module also instantiates `DEFAULT_CAPABILITY_REGISTRY` pre-populated with the three built-in capabilities (preset_speaker_tts, voice_description_tts, reference_voice_clone) and re-exports it through `core.contracts.__init__`. Aliases resolve through `get/is_supported` but are excluded from `names()` to avoid double-counting. Collisions on either name or execution_mode raise `ValueError` so the registry stays bijective across modes.

class-CapabilitySpec.PURPOSE: Frozen description of a single synthesis capability (name, execution_mode, description, aliases)

class-CapabilityRegistry.PURPOSE: Process-local registry that owns the capability <-> execution_mode mapping

M-HOST-PROBE.NAME: HostProbe
M-HOST-PROBE.purpose: Detect active OS, architecture, accelerators, and runtime features for capability-based planning.

M-BACKENDS.NAME: BackendRegistry
M-BACKENDS.purpose: Register, select, and manage TTS inference backends (MLX, accelerated Qwen fast, Torch, and ONNX).
M-BACKENDS.implementation-note: Torch inference is split between a thin dispatcher (`core/backends/torch_backend/dispatcher.py`) and per-family strategies under `core/backends/torch_backend/` (Qwen3TTSStrategy, OmniVoiceStrategy). Adding a new Torch family means adding a new TorchFamilyStrategy subclass and registering it on TorchBackend; no edits to the dispatcher are required.

class-BackendRegistry.PURPOSE: Backend selection and model spec resolution

class-QwenFastBackend.PURPOSE: Accelerated Qwen backend for custom, design, and clone with explicit CUDA/runtime gating

class-TorchBackend.PURPOSE: Thin PyTorch dispatcher that selects a TorchFamilyStrategy by family key

class-TorchFamilyStrategy.PURPOSE: Abstract per-family strategy contract: load_model_class plus synthesize_custom/design/clone

class-Qwen3TTSStrategy.PURPOSE: TorchFamilyStrategy for the Qwen3 family backed by qwen_tts.Qwen3TTSModel

class-OmniVoiceStrategy.PURPOSE: TorchFamilyStrategy for the OmniVoice family backed by omnivoice.OmniVoice

class-ONNXBackend.PURPOSE: ONNX runtime backend for Piper voices

class-LoadedModelHandle.PURPOSE: Handle to a loaded model with metadata

M-DISCOVERY.NAME: DiscoveryHelpers
M-DISCOVERY.purpose: Auto-discover concrete TTSBackend, ModelFamilyAdapter, and ModelFamilyPlugin classes from in-process __subclasses__() recursion plus optional importlib.metadata entry points so that bootstrap can wire backends and families without hardcoded imports.
M-DISCOVERY.implementation-note: Two discovery sources combined per call: (1) recursive `__subclasses__()` traversal of the relevant base class (`TTSBackend`, `ModelFamilyAdapter`, or `ModelFamilyPlugin`) which works whether or not the project is pip-installed; (2) `importlib.metadata.entry_points()` lookup against `tts_server.backends`, `tts_server.family_adapters`, and `tts_server.model_families` which lets external pip packages register their classes without editing `bootstrap.py`. Abstract classes are filtered out, results are deduplicated by fully qualified name, and sorted by `(declared key, qualified name)` for determinism. Phase 2.6 introduces these helpers; Phase 2.8 removes the hardcoded backend / family wiring from `bootstrap.py` by switching it to call these helpers.

fn-discover_backend_classes.PURPOSE: Enumerate concrete TTSBackend implementations (subclass scan + entry points)

M-MODEL-FAMILY.NAME: ModelFamilyAdapter
M-MODEL-FAMILY.purpose: Define family-level capability matching and execution preparation independent of transport adapters.
M-MODEL-FAMILY.implementation-note: Two coexisting extension seams during the migration to plugin-first architecture: the legacy `ModelFamilyAdapter` (`core/model_families/base.py`) which only owns planner preparation, and the unified `ModelFamilyPlugin` (`core/model_families/plugin.py`) which bundles availability, model loading, synthesis, and artifact validation behind a single contract. Phase 2.5 introduces `ModelFamilyPlugin` and the `FamilyPluginRegistry`; Phase 2.6 will wire entry-point discovery (`tts_server.model_families`); Phase 3 begins migrating adapter consumers onto the plugin surface.

class-ModelFamilyAdapter.PURPOSE: Legacy planner-side family adapter contract (capability + prepare_execution)

class-ModelFamilyPlugin.PURPOSE: Unified family extension contract (is_available, load_model, synthesize, validate_artifacts)

class-FamilyPluginRegistry.PURPOSE: Process-local registry that holds plugins and answers capability/backend lookups

class-FamilyExecutionRequest.PURPOSE: Frozen synthesis-input DTO handed to plugin.synthesize

class-FamilyExecutionResult.PURPOSE: Frozen synthesis-output DTO (waveforms + sample rate) returned by plugin.synthesize

M-QWEN3-FAMILY.NAME: Qwen3FamilyAdapter
M-QWEN3-FAMILY.purpose: Wrap current custom, design, and clone semantics as the first explicit model-family adapter.

M-OMNIVOICE-FAMILY.NAME: OmniVoiceFamilyAdapter
M-OMNIVOICE-FAMILY.purpose: Adapt OmniVoice custom, design, and clone semantics into planner-prepared backend execution inputs.

class-OmniVoiceFamilyAdapter.PURPOSE: Planner-facing family adapter for OmniVoice runtime semantics

M-INFRASTRUCTURE.NAME: Infrastructure
M-INFRASTRUCTURE.purpose: Provide local implementations for job execution, audio I/O, admission control, and concurrency.

class-LocalBoundedExecutionManager.PURPOSE: Local async job execution with bounded concurrency

fn-build_rate_limiter.PURPOSE: Factory for rate limiter from settings

fn-build_quota_guard.PURPOSE: Factory for quota guard from settings

M-MODEL-REGISTRY.NAME: ModelRegistry
M-MODEL-REGISTRY.purpose: Discover, validate, preload, and serve model handles through the selected backend.
M-MODEL-REGISTRY.depends: M-ARTIFACT-REGISTRY, M-BACKENDS, M-ERRORS, M-METRICS, M-MODEL-CATALOG, M-MODELS, M-OBSERVABILITY, M-PROFILE-RESOLVER, M-RUNTIME-MODEL-REGISTRY

class-ModelRegistry.PURPOSE: High-level model discovery and loading facade

M-MODEL-CATALOG.NAME: ModelCatalogRegistry
M-MODEL-CATALOG.purpose: Expose family-aware model metadata and stable public identifiers independent of local artifact presence.
M-MODEL-CATALOG.implementation-note: Family-aware metadata is exposed through the split catalog surface and remains independent from runtime artifact presence.

M-ARTIFACT-REGISTRY.NAME: ArtifactRegistry
M-ARTIFACT-REGISTRY.purpose: Resolve local artifacts and validate installed model assets separately from declared catalog metadata.
M-ARTIFACT-REGISTRY.implementation-note: Artifact-state inspection is isolated from runtime loading and backs readiness reporting directly.

class-ArtifactRegistry.PURPOSE: Registry for local model artifact resolution and validation

M-RUNTIME-MODEL-REGISTRY.NAME: RuntimeModelRegistry
M-RUNTIME-MODEL-REGISTRY.purpose: Manage runtime preload policy and descriptor-facing cache state independently from metadata and artifact discovery.
M-RUNTIME-MODEL-REGISTRY.implementation-note: Runtime state depends only on artifact inspection and the public preload report contract.

class-RuntimeModelRegistry.PURPOSE: Registry for loaded model handles and preload state

M-CAPABILITY-RESOLVER.NAME: CapabilityResolver
M-CAPABILITY-RESOLVER.purpose: Resolve which family, model, backend, and host combination can satisfy a requested capability.

class-CapabilityResolver.PURPOSE: Compatibility resolver with explicit rejection reasons

M-SYNTHESIS-PLANNER.NAME: SynthesisPlanner
M-SYNTHESIS-PLANNER.purpose: Resolve normalized synthesis requests into executable plans that preserve transport isolation.
M-SYNTHESIS-PLANNER.implementation-note: Planner resolution now relies on the registry contract as the single source of model lookup instead of carrying a direct manifest/catalog fallback, and the seam is typed through core/contracts/runtime.py.

class-SynthesisPlanner.PURPOSE: Planner for family, model, backend, and artifact resolution

M-BACKENDS-V2.NAME: ExecutionBackendContract
M-BACKENDS-V2.purpose: Own the direct backend execution contract and diagnostics surface for family-prepared runtime requests.

class-ExecutionRequest.PURPOSE: Backend execution request carrying family-prepared runtime inputs

class-QwenFastBackend.PURPOSE: Accelerated Qwen backend that remains additive to the direct execution contract for custom, design, and clone

M-TTS-SERVICE.NAME: TTSService
M-TTS-SERVICE.purpose: Coordinate inference for custom, design, and clone synthesis modes via the SynthesisRouter unified seam, while keeping the transport-facing TTSService.synthesize_X(...) facade stable.
M-TTS-SERVICE.depends: M-MODEL-REGISTRY, M-CONFIG, M-ERRORS, M-OBSERVABILITY, M-INFRASTRUCTURE, M-MODEL-FAMILY
M-TTS-SERVICE.implementation-note: Phase 3.9 introduces `core/services/synthesis_router.py::SynthesisRouter` as the unified Command -> backend seam. `TTSService.__init__` now builds a `SynthesisRouter(coordinator=...)` and `synthesize_custom`, `synthesize_design`, and `synthesize_clone` all delegate through `self.router.route_custom/_design/_clone(...)`. The router exposes a single public `route(command: GenerationCommand) -> GenerationResult` entry-point that dispatches by `isinstance(command, ...)`, raising `TTSGenerationError` with structured details when the command type is unsupported. Phase 3.11 then collapses the historic `generate_audio` free function: `SynthesisCoordinator._run_generation` now calls `backend.execute(ExecutionRequest(...))` directly inside a `START_BLOCK_DISPATCH_TO_BACKEND` block, the module-level `generate_audio(*args, **kwargs)` shim is deleted, and tests now substitute behaviour by overriding `registry.backend.execute` on a stable `_StubBackend` instead of monkeypatching `core.services.tts_service.generate_audio`. From the consumer's perspective this leaves the public pipeline at three perceived layers (TTSService -> SynthesisRouter -> backend) and removes one internal hop (SynthesisCoordinator -> backend instead of SynthesisCoordinator -> generate_audio -> backend).

class-TTSService.PURPOSE: Core synthesis coordinator with inference guard; delegates each synthesize_X call through SynthesisRouter

class-SynthesisRouter.PURPOSE: Phase 3.9 unified Command -> backend routing seam exposing route(command) plus per-mode helpers

class-SynthesisCoordinator.PURPOSE: Per-mode worker invoked by SynthesisRouter; calls backend.execute(ExecutionRequest) directly after Phase 3.11

M-STREAMING.NAME: AudioStreamingChunker
M-STREAMING.purpose: Provide a transport-agnostic seam that turns a completed GenerationResult into an iterator of fixed-size audio chunks so HTTP streaming endpoints (and future native-streaming backends) share one chunking contract.
M-STREAMING.implementation-note: Phase 4.12 introduces `core/services/streaming.py` with `iter_audio_chunks(payload, chunk_size)` (generic byte slicer that emits at least one boundary chunk for empty payloads and rejects non-positive chunk sizes) and `stream_generation_result(result, chunk_size)` (wraps each slice in an `AudioStreamChunk` carrying `index`, `total_chunks`, `data`, `final`, `media_type`, `model`, `mode`, and `backend`). The new `POST /api/v1/tts/custom/stream` route in `server/api/routes_tts.py` consumes the chunker through `StreamingResponse`, surfaces the chunk count via the `x-tts-stream-chunks` header alongside the existing `x-request-id`, `x-model-id`, `x-tts-mode`, and `x-backend-id` headers, and reuses `synthesize_custom(...)` so the backend-side execution pipeline is unchanged. Native-streaming backends can later replace `stream_generation_result` with a true async generator of decoded audio frames without changing the public route shape.

class-AudioStreamChunk.PURPOSE: Frozen container describing a single chunk of streamed audio plus its position metadata

fn-iter_audio_chunks.PURPOSE: Generic byte chunker that yields fixed-size byte slices from a bytes blob

fn-stream_generation_result.PURPOSE: Wrap a GenerationResult's audio payload as an iterator of AudioStreamChunk values with model/mode/backend metadata

M-TTS-COORDINATOR.NAME: SynthesisCoordinator
M-TTS-COORDINATOR.purpose: Refactor the current TTS service into a coordinator over planner resolution, family execution semantics, persistence, and observability.
M-TTS-COORDINATOR.implementation-note: Planner and coordinator seams depend on typed runtime registry protocols defined in core/contracts/runtime.py instead of concrete registry implementations.

class-TTSService.PURPOSE: Coordinator-shaped synthesis service over planning, family preparation, and runtime execution

M-APPLICATION.NAME: TTSApplicationService
M-APPLICATION.purpose: Provide application-level facade for TTS operations including job execution gateway and admission control.

class-TTSApplicationService.PURPOSE: High-level synthesis facade for transport adapters

M-BOOTSTRAP.NAME: CoreBootstrap
M-BOOTSTRAP.purpose: Assemble the full CoreRuntime from settings by wiring all components together via auto-discovery (TTSBackend subclasses + composite manifest) so adding a new backend or model never requires editing this module.
M-BOOTSTRAP.depends: M-CONFIG, M-BACKENDS, M-MODELS, M-DISCOVERY, M-MODEL-REGISTRY, M-TTS-SERVICE, M-APPLICATION, M-INFRASTRUCTURE, M-METRICS, M-PROFILES
M-BOOTSTRAP.implementation-note: Phase 2.8 removes the hardcoded backend list and `model_manifest_path` plumbing from `build_runtime`. The bootstrap now (1) imports the four built-in backend modules at module load time only to register their `TTSBackend` subclasses, (2) calls `core.discovery.discover_backend_classes()` to obtain every concrete `TTSBackend` subclass (in-process subclasses + `tts_server.backends` entry-points), (3) constructs each backend through the new `TTSBackend.from_settings(settings, *, metrics)` classmethod factory (overridden in `MLXBackend` to use `mlx_models_dir` and in `QwenFastBackend` to thread `qwen_fast_enabled`), skipping any backend whose constructor raises with a `[Bootstrap][build_backends][SKIP]` warning, and (4) loads the active manifest via `core.models.composite.load_composite_manifest(base_path=settings.model_manifest_path, models_dir=settings.models_dir)` and passes the pre-loaded `ModelManifest` into `BackendRegistry(..., model_manifest=...)` (a new keyword that takes precedence over the legacy `model_manifest_path`). The result is that `bootstrap.py` no longer enumerates concrete backend classes, no longer hardcodes per-backend constructor signatures, and transparently picks up per-family fragments and `.models/` auto-discovery.

class-CoreRuntime.PURPOSE: Frozen dataclass holding all wired runtime components

fn-build_runtime.PURPOSE: Factory function that assembles CoreRuntime from settings via auto-discovered backends and composite manifest

fn-build_backends.PURPOSE: Phase 2.8 helper: auto-discovers concrete TTSBackend subclasses and instantiates each through TTSBackend.from_settings(...)

M-DOCKER-ASSETS.NAME: DockerDeploymentAssets
M-DOCKER-ASSETS.purpose: Define the Windows-validated Docker Desktop deployment surface for the server and Telegram adapters, including compose scenarios, container trust-store wiring, and shared runtime mounts.

file-server-Dockerfile.PURPOSE: HTTP server container image definition with runtime directories, ffmpeg, and container CA trust updates

file-telegram-bot-Dockerfile.PURPOSE: Telegram bot container image definition with ffmpeg, state directory, and container CA trust updates

file-docker-compose-server.PURPOSE: Compose deployment for the FastAPI server with shared bind mounts and published port 8000

file-docker-compose-telegram.PURPOSE: Compose deployment for the Telegram bot with shared bind mounts and named state volume

file-proxy-root-cert.PURPOSE: Corporate proxy root certificate injected into Linux-container trust stores for Docker-hosted package installation

M-SERVER.NAME: HTTPServer
M-SERVER.purpose: Expose TTS capabilities through FastAPI HTTP endpoints with auth, rate limiting, error handling, and the canonical Phase 1 runtime and model host contract.

fn-request_context_middleware.PURPOSE: Bind request-scoped observability context and uniform API response metadata

M-SERVER.implementation-note: Phase 1 target state makes this module the only local runtime and model host; Telegram and CLI must not be described as alternate runtime hosts in shared docs.

M-FRONTEND-DEMO.NAME: FrontendDemo
M-FRONTEND-DEMO.purpose: Provide a standalone static one-page demo frontend that targets the TTS HTTP API from a separate root-level module.

file-styles-css.PURPOSE: Industrial Cyber-Noir styling for the standalone demo

file-app-js.PURPOSE: Browser-side readiness checks, preset loading, clone submission, playback, and download flow

M-TELEGRAM.NAME: TelegramBot
M-TELEGRAM.purpose: Expose TTS capabilities through Telegram bot commands as a remote HTTP client with allowlist, rate limiting, and delivery persistence.

fn-main.PURPOSE: Telegram bot entry point and polling loop

file-tests-integration-test_remote_teleg.PURPOSE: Process-separated canonical remote-client proof surface for Phase 1 split topology

file-tests-architecture-test_no_telegram.PURPOSE: Architecture gate proving Telegram startup and command routing have no normal-path local runtime fallback

M-TELEGRAM.implementation-note: In Phase 1, Telegram is a remote client of the canonical HTTP contract, so no local model-hosting implication should be inferred from this module; the dedicated remote-client and no-local-fallback verification surfaces enforce that boundary.

M-CLI.NAME: InteractiveCLI
M-CLI.purpose: Provide interactive local CLI for synthesis workflows with optional audio playback, outside the Phase 1 central-server target unless a blocker appears.

fn-main.PURPOSE: CLI entry point with interactive loop

M-CLI.implementation-note: CLI remains out of the Phase 1 target state and must not be used as evidence of local runtime hosting in the central-server split.

M-PROFILE-SCHEMA.NAME: RuntimeProfileSchema
M-PROFILE-SCHEMA.purpose: Define immutable host, family, module, and resolved-launch profile DTOs plus serialization helpers for launcher and operator tooling.

class-HostProfile.PURPOSE: Immutable host capability snapshot used during launch-profile resolution

class-FamilyProfile.PURPOSE: Immutable family profile carrying runtime pack and backend metadata

class-ModuleProfile.PURPOSE: Immutable module profile carrying entrypoint and transport metadata

class-ResolvedLaunchProfile.PURPOSE: Immutable resolved launch profile combining host, family, module, compatibility, backend, and metadata payloads

M-PROFILE-RESOLVER.NAME: RuntimeProfileResolver
M-PROFILE-RESOLVER.purpose: Resolve host, family, and module profiles into compatibility-aware launch plans with dependency-pack resolution, expected interpreter calculation, dedicated-env runtime probing, and resolved-profile assembly.

class-ProfileResolver.PURPOSE: Resolver that loads profile catalogs, evaluates compatibility, resolves pack refs, probes dedicated envs, and assembles resolved launch profiles

M-PROFILES.NAME: RuntimeProfilesPackage
M-PROFILES.purpose: Expose the public runtime-profile barrel surface for launcher and operator tooling at the package import boundary.

export-FamilyProfile.PURPOSE: Public package export for family profile DTOs

export-HostProfile.PURPOSE: Public package export for host profile DTOs

export-ModuleProfile.PURPOSE: Public package export for module profile DTOs

export-ProfileResolver.PURPOSE: Public package export for runtime profile resolution

export-ResolvedLaunchProfile.PURPOSE: Public package export for resolved launch profile payloads

M-LAUNCHER.NAME: RuntimeLauncher
M-LAUNCHER.purpose: Provide the profile-aware launcher CLI for inspect, plan-run, doctor, bootstrap-env, check-env, exec, create-env, and universal interactive launch runtime contours.

fn-parse_args.PURPOSE: Launcher CLI parser for inspect, plan-run, doctor, bootstrap-env, check-env, exec, create-env, and launch commands

fn-_interactive_launcher_command.PURPOSE: Resolve the platform-specific wrapper command used by the universal interactive launch entrypoint

fn-_interactive_launcher_env.PURPOSE: Build environment overrides for delegating the universal interactive launch entrypoint into existing wrappers

fn-main.PURPOSE: Launcher command entrypoint for profile inspection, env diagnostics, execution planning, and wrapper dispatch

M-WINDOWS-LAUNCHER.NAME: WindowsInteractiveLauncher
M-WINDOWS-LAUNCHER.purpose: Provide a Windows-only interactive PowerShell launcher that orchestrates profile-aware family environments, optional model downloads, and adapter startup.

fn-Main.PURPOSE: Interactive launcher entry point that prompts for service and model, prepares the family env, and starts the selected adapter

fn-Ensure-FamilyEnvironment.PURPOSE: Bridge PowerShell orchestration to launcher create-env and check-env flows

fn-Ensure-ModelAvailability.PURPOSE: Validate local model artifacts and run family-aware download strategies when assets are missing

M-WINDOWS-LAUNCHER-CMD.NAME: WindowsInteractiveLauncherCmd
M-WINDOWS-LAUNCHER-CMD.purpose: Provide a Windows CMD entrypoint that runs the interactive PowerShell launcher through an inline command string so MachinePolicy AllSigned does not block the supported guided launch flow.

fn-launch-windows-cmd.PURPOSE: Resolve the project root and execute launch-windows.ps1 content through powershell.exe -Command rather than PowerShell -File.

M-WINDOWS-LAUNCHER-BAT.NAME: WindowsInteractiveLauncherBat
M-WINDOWS-LAUNCHER-BAT.purpose: Provide a repository-root clickable Windows BAT entrypoint that delegates to the shared root-level Python launcher for the supported guided launch flow.

fn-launch-bat.PURPOSE: Resolve the repository-root Python launcher and delegate to it for double-click guided launch startup.

M-ROOT-LAUNCHER.NAME: RootUniversalLauncher
M-ROOT-LAUNCHER.purpose: Provide a repository-root cross-platform Python launcher entrypoint that delegates to the shared launcher dispatch command.

fn-main.PURPOSE: Execute python -m launcher launch from the repository root and return the delegated exit code.

M-ROOT-LAUNCHER-SH.NAME: RootUniversalLauncherShell
M-ROOT-LAUNCHER-SH.purpose: Provide a repository-root Unix shell wrapper for the shared root-level Python launcher entrypoint.

fn-launch-sh.PURPOSE: Resolve the repository root and exec the shared launch.py entrypoint through python3.

M-MACOS-LAUNCHER.NAME: MacOSInteractiveLauncher
M-MACOS-LAUNCHER.purpose: Provide a macOS-only interactive shell launcher that orchestrates profile-aware family environments, optional model downloads, and adapter startup.

fn-main.PURPOSE: Interactive macOS launcher entry point that prompts for service and model, prepares the family env, and starts the selected adapter

fn-assert-macos-preflight.PURPOSE: Validate macOS host prerequisites and offer bounded Homebrew install guidance for required system dependencies

fn-ensure-model-availability.PURPOSE: Validate local model artifacts and run guided Hugging Face or Piper download flows when assets are missing

M-LINUX-LAUNCHER.NAME: LinuxInteractiveLauncher
M-LINUX-LAUNCHER.purpose: Provide a Linux-only interactive shell launcher that orchestrates profile-aware family environments, optional model downloads, and adapter startup.

fn-main.PURPOSE: Interactive Linux launcher entry point that prompts for service and model, prepares the family env, and starts the selected adapter

fn-print-linux-install-guidance.PURPOSE: Emit package-manager-specific install commands for missing system dependencies without executing them

fn-ensure-model-availability.PURPOSE: Validate local model artifacts and run guided Hugging Face or Piper download flows when assets are missing
